methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Herasight is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Michael Christensen that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, though the narrow peer set limits applicability of certain methods.

Of the three triangulation approaches examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the $5M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median approach could not be applied due to insufficient comparable datapoints within tightly filtered bands (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier); while 14 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the minimum threshold of three comparables. Public-Comp Anchoring using the Biotech & Health sector P/S median of 6.1x also could not be executed, as Herasight's revenue is not on file—standard for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, centered at $12M. This range reflects limited triangulation depth; valuation confidence would substantially improve with either (1) audited or validated revenue figures to enable public-comp anchoring, or (2) access to 2–3 additional tightly comparable seed or early Series A financings. Key uncertainties include the company's current burn rate, clinical progress, and whether the $5M has been fully deployed.
